audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar altar-dedication offering text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for tabernacle dedication, transport gifts, equal tribal offerings across twelve days, altar dedication totals, and Divine voice above the ark.

verses.restored: On day Moses finished setting up dwelling-place, he anointed and sanctified it and all its vessels, altar and all its vessels; he anointed and sanctified them.
Leaders of Israel, heads of fathers' houses, leaders of tribes, those standing over the counted, brought near offerings.
They brought their offering before YHWH: six covered wagons and twelve oxen, a wagon for two leaders and an ox for each; they brought them before dwelling-place.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Take them from them, and they shall be for service of tent of meeting; give them to Levites, each according to his service.
Moses took wagons and oxen and gave them to Levites.
Two wagons and four oxen he gave to sons of Gershon according to their service.
Four wagons and eight oxen he gave to sons of Merari according to their service, under hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
But to sons of Kohath he gave none, because service of holy things was upon them; on shoulder they carried.
The leaders brought near dedication offering of altar on day it was anointed; leaders brought their offering before altar.
YHWH said to Moses, One leader each day, one leader each day, shall bring near his offering for dedication of altar.
The one bringing near on first day was Nahshon son of Amminadab, of tribe of Judah.
His offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Nahshon son of Amminadab.
On second day Nethanel son of Zuar, leader of Issachar, brought near.
He brought near his offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Nethanel son of Zuar.
On third day, leader of sons of Zebulun: Eliab son of Helon.
His offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Eliab son of Helon.
On fourth day, leader of sons of Reuben: Elizur son of Shedeur.
His offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Elizur son of Shedeur.
On fifth day, leader of sons of Simeon: Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
His offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
On sixth day, leader of sons of Gad: Eliasaph son of Deuel.
His offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Eliasaph son of Deuel.
On seventh day, leader of sons of Ephraim: Elishama son of Ammihud.
His offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Elishama son of Ammihud.
On eighth day, leader of sons of Manasseh: Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
His offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
On ninth day, leader of sons of Benjamin: Abidan son of Gideoni.
His offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Abidan son of Gideoni.
On tenth day, leader of sons of Dan: Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
His offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
On eleventh day, leader of sons of Asher: Pagiel son of Ocran.
His offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Pagiel son of Ocran.
On twelfth day, leader of sons of Naphtali: Ahira son of Enan.
His offering: one silver dish, weight one hundred thirty shekels; one silver basin, seventy shekels by sanctuary shekel; both full of fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering;
one gold pan, ten shekels, full of incense;
one bull, son of herd, one ram, one male lamb, son of a year, for burnt offering;
one male goat for sin/purification offering;
and for sacrifice of peace/well-being: two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs, sons of a year. This was offering of Ahira son of Enan.
This was dedication of altar, on day it was anointed, from leaders of Israel: twelve silver dishes, twelve silver basins, twelve gold pans.
Each silver dish weighed one hundred thirty, and each basin seventy; all silver of vessels was two thousand four hundred shekels by sanctuary shekel.
Twelve gold pans full of incense, ten shekels each by sanctuary shekel; all gold of pans was one hundred twenty shekels.
All cattle for burnt offering were twelve bulls, twelve rams, twelve male lambs, sons of a year, with their grain offerings, and twelve male goats for sin/purification offering.
All cattle for sacrifice of peace/well-being were twenty-four oxen, sixty rams, sixty male goats, and sixty male lambs, sons of a year. This was dedication of altar after it was anointed.
When Moses entered tent of meeting to speak with YHWH, he heard the voice speaking to him from above cover that was upon ark of testimony, from between two cherubim; and it spoke to him.

translation_notes: The repetition is intentional: each tribal leader receives the same narrative space and brings the same offering.
Transport gifts are distributed according to actual service burden; Kohath receives no wagons because the holy furnishings are carried on shoulders.
Equal offerings resist tribal superiority even though the leaders arrive on different days.
The chapter ends not with wealth totals but with Divine speech from above the ark.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 7 to glorify wealth, donor status, tribal prestige, or unequal spiritual access. The chapter emphasizes equal gifts, burden-matched resources, shared dedication, and Presence speaking at the center.
